pages.text: $(italic)Cadmus$() lets you $(bold)claim$() the ground you build on. A claimed chunk is yours: other players can't break, place, or steal within it, and — just as useful in single-player — you decide what's protected.$(br2)It is the quiet backbone of playing together without fences and locked doors.
pages.title: Claiming Land

pages.text: $(bold)The claim map.$() Press $(bold),$() (comma) to open Cadmus' map. Each square is a chunk; click to claim the ones you stand on and the ones around them, and your claim grows as a coloured patch you can see at a glance.$(br2)Every player has a $(italic)claim budget$() that grows with playtime — you can't fence off the whole world on day one, but a homestead is well within reach. $(italic)Partly-claimed land can also be set as a softer "party" zone for friends.$()
pages.title: The Claim Map

pages.text: $(bold)Why it's here.$() Novus is built to be shared (see $(italic)Waystones$(), and the server at large). Claims keep a co-op world friendly: your base stays your base whether or not anyone's watching it. In a solo world it's optional — claim your build to protect it from your own wandering creepers, or ignore it entirely.
pages.title: Why It's Here
